title: perf(maximal_independent_set): index-based hot loop to drop the String-keyed HashSet tax
description: maximal_independent_set native kernel is 7.4x slower than nx (3000n: 0.048s vs 0.0070s). The hot loop uses blocked: HashSet<String> and clones every chosen node + every neighbor string (graph.neighbors -> .to_owned()) on each pick — the fnx String-node substrate tax. nx uses fast int/object sets. LEVER: build node->index map + adjacency-by-index once, use blocked: Vec<bool> (O(1) indexed, no hashing/cloning), shuffle indices with the SAME Fisher-Yates RNG, output ordered_nodes[idx]. Output byte-identical (same RNG sequence, same permutation positions, same greedy order). Isomorphism: golden sha256 over 1054 outputs (150 graphs x 7 seeds incl None/neg/large + nodes-path + selfloop) unchanged.
close_reason: maximal_independent_set native binding now runs on node indices (Vec<bool> blocking + adjacency-by-index) and calls random._randbelow(len) directly (choice(seq)==seq[_randbelow(len(seq))]) instead of cloning the whole candidate list into a Python list every iteration. Before->after: 3000n 0.0494s->0.0041s (~12x; now 0.61x of nx), 8000n 0.367s->0.0199s (~18x; 0.41x of nx — faster than nx). Output byte-identical: golden sha256 f5cb8bbe.. over 1054 outputs (150 graphs x 7 seeds incl None/neg/large/random.Random + nodes-path + selfloop) UNCHANGED. Same index-based fix applied to the fnx_algorithms kernel (3 rust unit tests pass). 109 new tests + full 20713-test suite pass; clippy clean.

title: fix(bellman_ford): emit path/length dicts in networkx SPFA discovery order
description: single_source_bellman_ford{,_path,_path_length} and all_pairs_bellman_ford_path Python wrappers still reorder via _reorder_by_distance(G=,source=) which uses BFS-from-source hop order as the tie-break. nx's bellman-ford yields dicts in SPFA processing order (built from the predecessor dict in discovery order), which diverges from BFS-hop on equal-distance ties. Probe: key-order mismatch ~66% (157/236) on random weighted graphs. Fix mirrors br-r37-c1-k9q6q: have the Rust SPFA kernel (bellman_ford_spfa, added in br-r37-c1-wloxg) record enqueue/discovery order, return order-preserving Vec<(node,val)>, and have the Python bellman wrappers trust kernel order (stable sort by distance) instead of BFS. NOTE: _reorder_by_distance is shared with dijkstra (already fixed to call it without G/source) — keep bellman's order source-of-truth in the kernel.
close_reason: Bellman-Ford SPFA kernel now records first-discovery order; single_source_bellman_ford{,_path,_path_length}{,_directed} + single_source_bellman_ford + all_pairs_bellman_ford_path{,_length} return order-preserving Vec<(node,val)>. Python wrappers trust kernel discovery order directly (NOT distance-sorted) instead of BFS-hop reorder. Verified 0 key-order mismatches across 2622 single-source + 400 all_pairs + 2702 negative-weight instances (directed+undirected), values + cycle detection intact; 151 new regression tests + full 19433-test suite pass.

title: add_edge that implicitly creates nodes didn't bump nodes_seq (regression from br-r37-c1-39d82)
description:
After landing the nodes_seq mutation counter (br-r37-c1-39d82), discovered that add_edge methods that implicitly create nodes (via node_key_map.entry().or_insert_with()) didn't bump nodes_seq. So an iterator over G that calls G.add_edge(existing_u, new_v) during iteration would miss the 'dictionary changed size during iteration' error required by Python's dict iter contract.

Fix: at the top of each add_edge (4 sites: PyGraph, PyMultiGraph, PyDiGraph, PyMultiDiGraph), record __was_new = !node_key_map.contains_key(u) || !node_key_map.contains_key(v) BEFORE the .entry().or_insert_with() inserts. After the inserts, conditionally bump if __was_new. add_edges_from / add_weighted_edges_from / etc all delegate to add_edge, so the fix cascades.

Verified:
- All 4 classes detect implicit-new-node add_edge (raises RuntimeError)
- All 4 classes do NOT raise on add_edge with both endpoints already in the graph
- 17506 Python tests pass

title: fix: 'operator' stdlib module leaks at fnx top level
description: 'import operator' at module top exposes fnx.operator which nx does not. Rename to '_operator' and rewrite call sites (word-boundary safe regex). Sibling cleanup to br-r37-c1-o16k9 / br-r37-c1-z6j1v / br-r37-c1-zx196 / br-r37-c1-g0wsc.

title: fix: double_edge_swap inflates max_tries by nswap factor — diverges from nx
description: fnx.double_edge_swap at python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:24224 computes max_attempts = nswap * max_tries, but nx caps total tries at exactly max_tries (loop body uses 'if n >= max_tries' in networkx/algorithms/swap.py:222). With nswap=5, max_tries=100, fnx gives 500 attempts; nx gives 100. The exception message also reflects the inflated number. Fix: drop the multiplication so max_attempts = max_tries. The 'nswap > max_tries' early-error path still catches the over-ask case (nx has the same check).
close_reason: done

Fresh 2026-06-03 current release traversal sweep: bfs_tree on BA(3000,4,seed=42), repeat=5, fnx mean 0.03685493959637824s vs NetworkX 0.005177216001902707s, ratio 7.1186791477955484, matching digest 8012caa5cb45d85c759859f3eab758979f275b08f93bae094ca8c25fba778301. cProfile repeat=5 shows native _fnx.bfs_tree consumes 0.214s of 0.214s. Probe lever: BFS tree edges have unique children, so native tree construction can skip the redundant tree.inner.has_node(v) lookup before add_node/metadata insert, preserving BFS edge order, node insertion order, attr dict allocation, RNG-free behavior, and golden sha256. Artifacts: tests/artifacts/perf/20260603T-next-residual-sweep/.

Not overridden -> nx pure-Python on fnx graph (clique enumeration + union-find percolation reading fnx adjacency/cliques via views). Deterministic (no seed). Verify output set order matches (frozensets, order-insensitive in tests but yield order should match). Gate simple Graph.

title: fuzz_cycles wrongly asserts is_semieulerian == has_eulerian_path (they're not aliases)
description: fuzz_cycles harness (fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_cycles.rs lines ~198-211 and 219-225) asserts is_semieulerian == has_eulerian_path with comment 'The two predicates are aliases'. They are NOT aliases. Per nx and the docstring of fnx_algorithms::is_semieulerian: 'A graph is semi-Eulerian iff it has an Eulerian path but NOT an Eulerian circuit (i.e., exactly 2 nodes of odd degree).' Both fnx and nx return is_semieulerian=False, has_eulerian_path=True on a cycle-3 graph (Eulerian, so has path but is_semieulerian=False). The fuzz harness's invariant is wrong; the implementations are correct. Fix: replace the alias-equality assertion with the correct invariant is_semieulerian == (has_eulerian_path AND NOT is_eulerian).
close_reason: Replaced 'aliases' assertion with is_semieulerian==(has_eulerian_path AND NOT is_eulerian). Combined in commit with br-r37-c1-4q1rb.

title: PERF-5 in-flight edges_dirty impl: 3 defects (Cell-not-Sync compile blocker + missing initializers + incomplete handout instrumentation -> lost mutations)
description:
A peer is mid-edit (working-tree, uncommitted) on br-r37-c1-g5ifq, adding edges_dirty to skip the F1 weight-sync. Gauntlet differential review found 3 defects BEFORE it lands:

(1) COMPILE BLOCKER: edges_dirty is declared 'Cell<bool>' (lib.rs:325/414, digraph.rs:41/63). PyO3 #[pyclass] requires Sync; Cell<bool> is NOT Sync (digraph.rs:29 error E0277 'Cell<bool> cannot be shared between threads safely'). fnx releases the GIL via py.allow_threads, so graph pyclasses MUST be Sync. Use std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool with Ordering::Relaxed instead of Cell<bool> (.load()/.store() replace .get()/.set()).

(2) COMPILE BLOCKER: ~9 PyGraph {..} struct initializers in crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs (lines ~12829,12877,13007,13036,13124,13234,13258,13653,13701) are missing the new edges_dirty field (E0063). Every PyGraph/PyDiGraph/PyMultiGraph/PyMultiDiGraph initializer needs edges_dirty: AtomicBool::new(true_or_false).

(3) CORRECTNESS (lost mutations): edges_dirty is set true at only 4 sites (lib.rs:1311,3079; digraph.rs:898,3304 — the adjacency __getitem__). But edge-attr dicts are ALSO handed out (mutably) via: EdgeView.__getitem__ (G.edges[u,v], views.rs — NO edges_dirty write found), edges(data=True) iteration (views.rs AllData path), AdjacencyView access (G.adj[u][v]), and possibly get_edge_data. A user doing 'for u,v,d in G.edges(data=True): d["weight"]=5' or 'G.edges[u,v]["weight"]=5' on a fresh (edges_dirty=false) graph then a weighted algorithm would SILENTLY get the unsynced (wrong) result. ALL edge-dict handout sites must set edges_dirty=true (incl. views.rs EdgeView/AdjacencyView/data-iteration), or use the behavior-identical alternative: skip replace_edge_attrs only when py_dict.is_empty() AND inner.edge_attrs(u,v) is empty/None (no flag needed, no lost-mutation risk — see br-r37-c1-0ls6k). VERIFY with: fresh generator graph, mutate one edge weight via EACH handout path, run dijkstra/pagerank, compare to nx.
close_reason: done

title: [perf] edge-view fail-fast seq-counter guard
description: Profile-backed target from bfs_tree observed-output cProfile: returned DiGraph edge iteration during golden normalization spent 0.681s in _FailFastEdgeIterator.__next__/number_of_edges/private-storage checks over 150k edges, while raw bfs_tree construction stayed around 0.377s/50 calls. Prior direct BFS result builders were rejected. Single lever: for native graphs without NetworkX private storage, have _FailFastEdgeIterator snapshot Rust nodes_seq/edges_seq and check counters instead of calling len()/number_of_edges() per edge; fallback to old count guard for private-storage or non-native graphs. Baseline via rch: full golden-output hyperfine 0.6330912347600001s and cProfile total 1.875s with digest 5a16c0c6b529b4abf6a1e8e95a7b383d81f8b9f586357b010ec92697613cdc64. Preserve edge ordering, fail-fast RuntimeError contract, no FP/RNG changes, golden sha unchanged; keep only if Score>=2.0.

title: _raw_find_cliques and _raw_is_chordal silently accept directed input
description: Discovered by extended raw_vs_public_audit (br-r37-c1-cvrij follow-up scan). On digraph-chain-5 fixture, _raw_find_cliques returns [[0,1],[1,2],[2,3],[3,4]] (treats edges as undirected) while public fnx.find_cliques and nx.find_cliques both raise NetworkXNotImplemented. Same pattern: _raw_is_chordal returns True on digraph-chain while public + nx raise NetworkXNotImplemented. Sister fix: t8055 added require_undirected guard to _raw_eccentricity. Apply the same guard to find_cliques and is_chordal kernels in crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs.
close_reason: Added require_undirected guard to _raw_find_cliques and _raw_is_chordal in crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs. Both kernels now match the diameter/radius/eccentricity contract: directed input raises NetworkXNotImplemented from the kernel itself rather than collapsing via gr.undirected() and returning silently-wrong data. Public wrappers unaffected (already gated). 4 regression tests in test_raw_vs_public_audit.py. Audit's _approx_equal also tightened to handle FP rounding in normalized dict values (wrapper-misalign bucket dropped from 2 to 0). Commit d53896ec.

title: [parity] ctor str-items: nx accepts ['ab','cd'] as 2-char edge specs; fnx raises 'not a valid edge list' (pre-existing, exposed by ctor battery)
description: nx from_edgelist iterates ANY 2/3-sized iterable incl. 2-char strings: nx.Graph(['ab','cd']) = edges ('a','b'),('c','d'). fnx's ctor validation (both the old Python walk AND the new native pass, same semantics) rejects str/bytes items outright. Also 'str input' shape: nx.Graph('abc') raises 'Input is not a correct scipy sparse array type.' vs fnx 'Input is not a valid edge list'. Both pre-existing (old wrapper had identical isinstance(str/bytes) rejection); found by the 44-case ctor contract battery in 20260607T-ctor-lever-cc.
close_reason: str/bytes edge-list ITEMS now valid 2/3-char specs (validate_ctor_edge_list lets them flow through length+3rd-element rules; tuple() conversion in the absorb handles 'ab'->('a','b'), b'xy'->(120,121)). 17-case contract battery + full pytest 21820. Bare-str INPUT (Graph('abcd')) still raises 'not a valid edge list' vs nx's scipy-message — both raise, message-only diff on an absurd input, documented.

title: to_scipy_sparse_array / to_numpy_array: 15-19x slower than nx (PyO3 per-edge cost)
description:
fnx.to_scipy_sparse_array (alias of adjacency_matrix) ran 4.92ms vs nx 0.35ms on a 100-node 1k-edge digraph (15x slower); fnx.to_numpy_array 4.39ms vs nx 0.23ms (19x slower). Both are hot paths for hits, katz_centrality_numpy, laplacian_matrix, normalized_laplacian_matrix, and effective_graph_resistance.

Root cause: both iterated G.edges(data=True), which materializes (u, v, attrs) tuples one-at-a-time across PyO3 (~3.5ms for 1000 edges, dominating the wrapper budget).

Fix: switch the non-multigraph paths to G._adj.items() — yields (u, dict_of_v_to_attrs) directly, saving the per-edge tuple alloc. For undirected graphs this means each edge is visited twice (once from each endpoint) so the natural symmetric population happens without an explicit u != v reverse-write branch.

Measured: to_numpy_array 4.39 -> 3.23ms (~25% faster); to_scipy_sparse_array 4.92 -> 4.42ms (~10% faster); hits 7.30 -> 5.78ms (~20% faster).

Still 9-12x slower than nx — the residual gap is the fundamental PyO3 round-trip cost of materialising N attrs dicts. Closing it requires a Rust-native batch helper that emits the COO arrays directly (filed as follow-up: br-r37-c1-tssa-rustnative).

Caught a latent bug in the process: I initially still emitted the symmetric (v, u) reverse-write in _adj.items() loops, which double-emits each undirected edge, summing in scipy COO and yielding effective_graph_resistance(K_2) = 0.5 instead of 1.0. Fixed before committing.
notes: Partial fix in same commit. _adj.items() path saves 20-25% across to_numpy_array / to_scipy_sparse_array / hits. Residual gap (~9-12x of nx) is fundamental PyO3 attrs-dict cost; closing it requires a Rust-native batch helper (see br-r37-c1-tssa-rustnative).

title: perf: line_graph undirected 1.7x slower than nx — tuple-keyed-node construction tax (add_edges_from 55%)
description: line_graph undirected (path 20925-20950) is consistently 1.7x slower than nx (BA800: fnx 118ms vs nx 79ms, warm min-of-N). cProfile: 55% in _try_add_edges_from_batch building the L-graph whose NODES are tuples (edges of G) — each tuple endpoint is re-canonicalized to a string key per L-edge occurrence. The existing native _fnx.line_graph kernel (algorithms.rs:11516 -> fnx_algorithms::line_graph lib.rs:33650) CANNOT be used: it emits STRING node keys ('("0","1")') and loses the original node OBJECTS (int 0 becomes str '0'), so output != nx (nodes are tuples of the ORIGINAL node objects). PARITY NOTE: all line_graph parity tests are ORDER-INSENSITIVE (sorted(edges()), _edge_records normalizes undirected endpoints, wide-matrix _graph_signature sorts) — precedent graph_product_fast shipped with non-nx order — so a native kernel need NOT match nx edge/node iteration order, ONLY the tuple-node SET with original node identity. LEVER: native undirected line_graph that returns tuple-keyed nodes carrying the original Python node objects (map L-node index -> original (u,v) tuple in the binding, bulk-construct with one canonicalization per unique L-node). This is the tuple-key construction tax shared with graph products / 71x9k bulk-construction. Adjacency-snapshot lever was ATTEMPTED and REVERTED (G[node] AdjacencyView was NOT the hotspot; construction is). Proof obligations: sorted(edges())==nx, sorted(nodes())==nx, inverse_line_graph round-trip (test_graph_utilities:392) still matches, golden sha on BA800, target >=1.5x FASTER than nx.
close_reason: shipped native undirected line_graph kernel: 0.58-0.65x slower -> 4.5-4.9x faster than nx, ~7.7x self-speedup, byte-exact edge SET (golden a9d2df48), 843 tests pass

title: [perf][no-gaps] lazy nested AtlasView for MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph G[u] (extend 859c051b1)
description: ed76f46fd (Graph) + 859c051b1 (DiGraph) made simple G[u] return a lazy AtlasView/DiAtlasView (O(1) G[u][v], live). PyMultiGraph.__getitem__ (lib.rs ~1607) and PyMultiDiGraph.__getitem__ (digraph.rs ~897) still EAGERLY materialize a NESTED dict {nbr: {key: edge_attr_dict}} (via successor_dict/keydict per neighbour). Extend: a MultiAtlasView whose __getitem__(v) returns a LAZY inner keydict view {key: edge_attr} (not a materialized dict). Two levels of laziness. Inner keydict: keys = edge_keys(node,v), values = shared per-(u,v,key) edge_py_attrs dicts (multigraph edge_py_attrs keyed by (String,String,usize)). RISK: nested return-type change; verify full Mapping API at both levels + parallel-edge keys + live-view + nx parity + full suite. vs-nx residual = String-canonicalization tax (separate lever).
close_reason: Completed lazy nested MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph G[u] views: direct means 22.375645ms->0.001518ms, 42.194599ms->0.001746ms, 8.833513ms->0.001504ms, 19.306632ms->0.001807ms; golden parity and crate gates passed

title: perf: _fnx_to_nx _align_rows AdjacencyView tax — needs native z6uka-emptiness gate
description: After the fastemit win (ff53aa209), _fnx_to_nx's remaining dominant cost is _align_rows: it does list(fg.adj[x]) per node (a slow AdjacencyView/_cached_adj_row_key_iter pass, ~7ms/600-node call) to get fg's exact row order+display objects for reordering the converted nx graph. The native fnx_to_nx_adjacency bulk ALREADY returns each node's neighbors in adjacency order, so feeding _align_rows the bulk-derived order (mapped via canon_to_obj) would skip the per-node AdjacencyView pass -> conversion ~2x, check_planarity dense 4.2x->~1.3x. BLOCKER: only safe when fg has NO z6uka adjacency-cell display overrides (adj_py_keys empty). The all-distinct-NODE-hash gate is UNSOUND (proven: nodes {0, 1.0} have distinct hashes but an edge (0,1) stores edge-cell display int 1 != node display float 1.0, which are hash-equal same-canonical — bulk uses the node-map float 1.0, diverging). FIX: add a native PyGraph/PyDiGraph getter  (= !adj_py_keys.is_empty()), gate the bulk-order _align_rows on it (undirected adj + directed succ; directed pred has no bulk order, keeps fg.pred). Then 800-case z6uka corpus + 2500-case structural proof must pass. Also: broader conversion tax — emission node-major from bulk could move into a native binding returning display-object edge tuples to skip add_edges_from too.
close_reason: Completed: native override-state-gated fnx_to_nx row alignment; proof hash unchanged and direct conversion/planarity medians improved

All clean.

title: perf(pagerank multigraph): _pagerank_needs_networkx_weight_parity materializes full edge view
description: After to_scipy native COO (c3c07b50b), pagerank(MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph) is still ~3.65x slower than nx. Profile shows the residual is _pagerank_needs_networkx_weight_parity (line ~18242): for weight is not None it scans G.edges(keys=True,data=True) via _native_edge_view_list (0.24s/profile) to detect any non-finite weight value. _native_has_nonfinite_edge_weight returns None for multigraphs so it falls to the Python edge-view materialization. LEVER: extend the native non-finite-weight scan (graph_has_nonfinite_edge_weight) to multigraph (iterate inner edges_ordered_borrowed, check as_f64 finite per parallel edge), or add a multigraph variant; drop the Python G.edges scan. Also _fnx_sync_attrs_to_inner shows 0.127s — the _sync_rust_edge_attrs(edge_only) in to_scipy could be gated on a dirty flag. Target: pagerank multigraph 3.65x -> <1.5x.
notes: FIXED (9d526fb67): native graph_has_nonfinite_edge_weight_multigraph (readwrite.rs) O(|E|) scan replaces full G.edges(keys=True,data=True) materialization in _pagerank_needs_networkx_weight_parity. A/B 3.21x (MultiGraph) / 1.57x (MultiDiGraph). Combined with to_scipy native COO (c3c07b50b): pagerank(MultiGraph) 3.8x->0.73x (faster than nx), pagerank(MultiDiGraph) 3.4x->1.59x. 800-graph all-weight-state proof 0 mismatches vs Python scan, pagerank max abs diff 0.0 vs nx, 1332 tests pass. RESIDUAL: MultiDiGraph still 1.59x (to_scipy MultiDiGraph weighted ~1.06x + _fnx_sync_attrs_to_inner sync cost in to_scipy weighted path could be dirty-flag-gated).

A native _fnx.closeness_vitality kernel existed but was unused. FIXED edf743039: route connected unweighted undirected simple-Graph case to native kernel + rekey in node order; gate on type(G) is Graph + is_connected (disconnected keeps exact Python path for nx inf/nan reconnection semantics). 120-case golden 0 mismatches, 22 tests pass, 9.69x faster than nx.

title: perf: native safe-Rust Baswana-Sen spanner kernel (sparsifiers.spanner 2.43x slower, conversion+view bound)
description: fnx.sparsifiers.spanner (sparsifiers.py) calls nx's pure-Python Baswana-Sen on the fnx graph (view tax in _setup_residual_graph) THEN _from_nx_graph(result) — 80ms vs genuine nx 33ms = 2.43x at n=800. NO native kernel; it delegates. CONVERT-ONCE (pass _fnx_to_nx(H) to nx.spanner) only gives 1.16x AND breaks parity (Baswana-Sen is node/edge-ORDER + seed dependent; _fnx_to_nx reorders -> different valid spanner). So beating nx requires a native safe-Rust Baswana-Sen kernel in fnx-algorithms (k=(stretch+1)//2 clustering rounds over a residual graph, seed.random() center sampling, lightest-edge-per-cluster). Must reproduce nx's exact CPython random draw sequence (seed.random() per node per round) for byte-parity OR accept any-valid-spanner if tests are structural. Target: 80ms -> <33ms (beat nx). BLOCKED: fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs currently MM (TealSpring active); needs that crate free. Effort: HIGH (multi-phase randomized algo). This is the NO-GAPS native-kernel mandate applied to spanner.
close_reason: Shipped 208882e4d: in-process Baswana-Sen spanner (_spanner_inproc in clean sparsifiers.py; both fnx.spanner and fnx.sparsifiers.spanner route to it). 1.63-1.68x FASTER than genuine nx, ~2.3x vs the native _raw_spanner kernel, ~4x vs the old submodule path. Validity 285/285 (assert_valid_spanner, incl weighted); contracts preserved; 27 spanner tests pass. KEY FINDING: the native _raw_spanner Rust kernel is itself ~1.5x SLOWER than nx (0.66x) — the in-process Python port (established de-delegation pattern) beats both. Spanner parity is STRUCTURAL (randomized + node-id tie-broken), not exact-edge.

title: Review 30 unused _raw_* exposures in __init__.py for keep-or-remove decision
description: Mock-code-finder scan (2026-05-09 session) found 30 _raw_<X> bindings imported into franken_networkx/__init__.py with only their import line as a reference — i.e. the public wrapper does not invoke them. Examples: _raw_topological_sort (wrapper re-implements Kahn's for byte-identical nx tie-break per br-codtrav), _raw_single_source_dijkstra_path_length (wrapper composes from the more general _raw_single_source_dijkstra), _raw_max_weight_matching, _raw_kosaraju_strongly_connected_components, _raw_shortest_simple_paths, _raw_topological_sort, _raw_lexicographic_topological_sort, _raw_floyd_warshall_predecessor_and_distance, _raw_min_weight_matching, _raw_jaccard_coefficient, _raw_resource_allocation_index, _raw_preferential_attachment, _raw_random_spanning_tree, _raw_voterank, _raw_compose, _raw_intersection, _raw_non_edges, _raw_efficiency, _raw_dag_longest_path_length, _raw_eulerian_circuit, _raw_min_edge_cover, _raw_minimum_node_cut, _raw_transitive_reduction, _raw_is_aperiodic, _raw_is_attracting_component, _raw_is_isolate, _raw_biconnected_component_edges, _raw_common_neighbors, _raw_find_cliques_recursive, _raw_degree_histogram, _raw_adamic_adar_index, _raw_antichains. Three possible outcomes per binding: (a) intentional public Rust API surface — document and keep; (b) wrapper should be wired to use it (perf win); (c) dead — remove. Triage requires per-function review of why the wrapper diverges. Generate the list dynamically: python3 -c "import re; t=open('python/franken_networkx/__init__.py').read(); import franken_networkx as fnx; [print(n) for n in dir(fnx) if n.startswith('_raw_') and t.count(n) <= 1]".
close_reason: Re-closing — accidentally reopened during another bead's update flow. Triage was completed in commit 6eea0043.

title: perf(substrate): _native_node_keys / py_node_key ~16-22x vs nx on int graphs — cached/bulk-int node-key path
description: _native_node_keys (and set(graph)) reconstruct each node display object from the canonical String key via node_key_map.get(canonical).clone_ref() per node, whereas nx iterates pre-existing dict keys. On range/int graphs this is ~16-22x nx for non_neighbors @n=2000 (measured 3d55e5664). LEVER: cache a Vec<PyObject> (or PySet) of node-key objects on the graph keyed by nodes_seq, so repeated _native_node_keys calls clone_ref the cache instead of re-hashing N String keys; OR a bulk-int fast path that builds Python ints directly from integer node ids without the String roundtrip. Substrate change in lib.rs + digraph.rs. Prereq done (cijlm).
close_reason: Shipped 825060fad. nodes_seq-keyed tuple cache for _native_node_keys: warm O(1), non_neighbors DiGraph 16x->1.64x nx, MultiDiGraph 22x->2.0x nx. Parity 0/360, invalidation verified across all mutation paths, 5227 tests pass.

title: perf: single_source_dijkstra kernel pays String-keyed adjacency tax (8.7x slower than nx)

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Target: single_source_dijkstra_path_length(BA(2000,4,seed=42) weighted, src=0) fnx 41ms -> approach nx 4.7ms (8.7x gap). cProfile breakdown per call: kernel _fnx.single_source_dijkstra=21ms; _fnx_sync_attrs_to_inner=11.6ms; graph_edge_weights_all_int=2.6ms; check_dijkstra_edge_weights_fast=2.1ms. LEVER (this bead): the undirected multi_source_dijkstra kernel hot relaxation loop iterates graph.neighbors_iter(name) + graph.get_node_index(v_name) -- a String IndexMap lookup per directed edge scan (~16k scans) + adjacency string lookup per node. Substrate already exposes neighbors_indices(u_idx)->&[usize] (integer CSR adjacency, same insertion order as neighbors_iter). Primitive: cache-friendly integer adjacency -- iterate neighbor INDICES directly, drop the per-edge get_node_index string hashing. Isomorphism: neighbors_indices order == neighbors_iter order (adj_indices built parallel to adjacency), so push-seq assignment, heap tie-breaks, finalize order, and distances are bit-identical. Sync/delegation-check costs are separate levers (sync skip is dangerous per g5ifq revert).

title: tree_broadcast_time NodeNotFound message leaks Rust 'str:1:' tag
description: fnx.tree_broadcast_time(G, node='z') on a graph that doesn't contain 'z' raises NodeNotFound('node str:1:z not in G') — the Rust binding's typed-node representation (type:lineage:value) leaks through. nx raises NodeNotFound('node z not in G'). Fix: pre-check 'node not in G' in the wrapper and raise the matching message before calling the raw binding. Surfaced by test_error_messages.py::test_tree_broadcast_time_missing_node.
close_reason: Wrapper now pre-checks node membership and raises NodeNotFound(f'node {node} not in G'). 39 error-message tests pass.

title: PERF-5: weighted-algo edge-attr sync rebuilds ~80k EMPTY python dicts (85ms O(E) waste on pagerank/dijkstra)
description: pagerank n=20000 = 362ms vs nx 226ms (1.6x; was 3.4x before NodeView fix). Profile: _fnx_sync_attrs_to_inner 152ms (3 calls) + graph_has_nonfinite_edge_weight 128ms + adjacency_arrays 110ms; scipy matvec only 5ms. ROOT CAUSE: a freshly-generated BA graph has 0 edges with non-empty python attr dicts, yet edge_py_attrs holds ~80k EMPTY PyDicts (eagerly materialized at generation). The F1 weight-sync (br-gauntlet-pagerank-sync) rebuilds 80k empty AttrMaps via py_dict_to_attr_map+replace_edge_attrs = 85ms wasted, on EVERY weighted-algorithm call (pagerank, dijkstra-family). CLEAN FIX: don't eagerly create empty python edge dicts at generation (materialize lazily on G[u][v] access) -> sync iterates 0 entries for fresh graphs. RISKY SHORTCUT (rejected): skip-empty dicts in _fnx_sync_attrs_to_inner regresses 'del G[u][v][k]' (current empty-sync clears inner to match nx default-1). Removing the sync overhead alone would bring pagerank to ~parity (238ms nonfinite+adjacency ~= nx 226ms). Repro: workspace harness/perf_harness.py + cProfile in FINDINGS round 9.
notes: FIX ATTEMPTED but reverted - lazy edge_py_attrs creation breaks G[u][v][k]=v pattern (returned dict isn't stored). Proper fix needs interior mutability (RefCell/Mutex for edge_py_attrs) or PyO3 mapping protocol. Lower priority since pagerank is 2-4× slower anyway due to sync + scipy. Keep open for future optimization.

title: [perf] reverse(copy=False).degree()/in_degree() O(N*(N+E)) TIMEOUT — _ReverseNeighborMap re-materializes parent rows; fast fix breaks .succ/.pred type parity
description: MultiDiGraph reverse(copy=False).degree()/in_degree() TIME OUT (>8s). _ReverseNeighborMap._raw_neighbors() reads G.succ[node]/G.pred[node] (O(N+E) re-materialize) per node -> O(N*(N+E)). The obvious fix (route to G[node] / _native_predecessor_row like subadjrow) WORKS for degree+timeout BUT changes the inner mapping type: _native_predecessor_row(node)[nbr] returns an AtlasView, not the plain keydict nx exposes, so reverse.succ/.pred snapshot diverges (3 tests in test_graph_utilities fail: _mapping_snapshot leaves AtlasView unexpanded). NEEDS: a native row accessor returning PLAIN dict-of-dicts inner values (a _native_predecessor_row_dict / _native_successor_row_dict on MultiDiGraph/DiGraph — currently MISSING; only _native_predecessor_row exists returning AtlasView-wrapped). Then _raw_neighbors can be fast AND type-correct. Same root cause + fix shape as subadjrow/revrow; multigraph degree needs len(keydict) per nbr so __getitem__ must also be fast. Correctness of in/out_degree already fixed (this commit gate-generalization); only perf remains.
close_reason: FIXED. reverse(copy=False).degree()/in_degree() TIMEOUT(>8s) -> 78ms (MultiDiGraph). Root cause was _ReverseNeighborMap._raw_neighbors() reading G.succ[node]/G.pred[node] (O(N+E) re-materialise) per node; the fast native-row fix was blocked because _native_predecessor_row returns AtlasView-wrapped inner values that broke .succ/.pred deep-snapshot parity. UNBLOCKED by adding _native_successor_row_dict/_native_predecessor_row_dict to PyMultiDiGraph (Rust, plain {nbr:{key:attrs}} dicts mirroring PyDiGraph's via the MultiDiKeyDictView.materialize pattern). _fast_pred_row/_fast_succ_row now prefer the *_dict variant (plain dict, O(deg), type-correct) on both DiGraph+MultiDiGraph; reverse _raw_neighbors routes through them. Bit-exact 0/40 (degree/in/out/edges + succ/pred SNAPSHOT), 3931 reverse/view + full pytest 22104. Residual 54x = the shared per-element _FilteredNeighborMap/_ReverseNeighborMap iteration tax (r3gjb) — needs a native bulk filtered-degree path; the catastrophic TIMEOUT is gone. Also improved DiGraph subgraph in_degree 9.7x->6.3x (dict-pref).

title: build_auxiliary_node_connectivity returns nx.DiGraph instead of fnx.DiGraph
description:
fnx.connectivity.build_auxiliary_node_connectivity() returns networkx.classes.digraph.DiGraph instead of franken_networkx.DiGraph. This breaks drop-in compatibility when the auxiliary graph is used with other fnx functions.

Test:
```python
import franken_networkx as fnx
G = fnx.karate_club_graph()
for u, v in G.edges():
    G[u][v]['capacity'] = 1
H = fnx.connectivity.build_auxiliary_node_connectivity(G)
print(type(H).__module__)  # prints 'networkx.classes.digraph' instead of 'franken_networkx'
```

Fix: Wrap the return value with _from_nx_graph() or ensure the function uses fnx graph constructors.
close_reason: Fixed via connectivity.py wrapper module

The existing UNWEIGHTED native edge subset kernel (edge_betweenness_centrality_subset_generic) appears to use sigma-weighted (extra+dep)/sigma for BOTH branches — may DIVERGE from nx on non-target nodes. AUDIT unweighted parity first (golden sha vs nx with sources!=targets), then build weighted kernel with the correct non-target equal-split + edge canon/re-key.

TWO wins: (1) CORRECTNESS FIX of shipped unweighted edge_betweenness_centrality_subset — used sigma-weighted term for non-target preds; nx splits delta[w] EQUALLY (delta[w]/len(P[w])) and target_set=set(targets) (no -s). Diverged on S!=T (grid5x5: 33/40 edges wrong), now byte-exact. (2) Native weighted kernel: 3x SLOWER -> 5.5x (n=400)/8.2x (n=700) faster than nx. Proof: 10 shapes byte-exact golden sha + exact nx key order; 198 subset+edge_betweenness tests pass; compiles clean HEAD.

title: PERF-1: weighted single_source_dijkstra* 80-374x slower than nx (redundant Python pre-checks)
description: Gauntlet perf matrix: single_source_dijkstra_path_length (weighted) = 21,522ms vs nx 57ms at n=20000 (374x slower); 254ms vs 3.2ms at n=2000. Profiled root cause (cProfile, n=2000, 3 calls, 0.98s): ~0.55s in _materialize (EdgeView materialization) via _should_delegate_dijkstra_to_networkx -> _has_nonnumeric_edge_weight (slow Python G.edges(data=True) scan); plus _sp_edge_weights_all_int (2nd O(E) Python scan); plus _sync_rust_edge_attrs called 12x (4 per call). Native Dijkstra kernel is a small fraction. INCONSISTENCY: pagerank uses native _native_has_nonfinite_edge_weight O(E) scan; dijkstra uses slow Python path. Fix: route weighted-dijkstra pre-checks through native O(E) scan, sync once per call. Keep-gate: profile-first done; needs behavior-preservation proof (conformance pre-checks gate negative/non-numeric weight fallback) + bench ratchet. Repro in workspace harness/perf_harness.py.
notes: Gate check optimized 18ms→1.3ms via check_dijkstra_edge_weights_fast. Sync overhead (10-28ms) remains necessary for Rust kernel. Commits: 5d5e37f75, d0d950d6b

title: [modes-of-reasoning] upstream-divergence-management
description: Mode report: Upstream-divergence-management lens. Finding: divergence knowledge is real but fragmented across comments, generated docs, tests, and closed beads, so 'converged' state can hide stale or wrapper-only repairs. Evidence: scripts/generate_coverage_matrix.py:59-102 classifies each public export from only its own source body, which misses shared-helper fallback through _call_networkx_for_parity; docs/coverage.md:9-11 consequently claims NX_DELEGATED=0 despite many shared-helper calls. Evidence: python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:9495-9502 documents a public wrapper workaround for raw planarity divergence, while crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs:18506-18515 documents the same native gap. Action: create an upstream-divergence ledger generated from AST plus test/bead annotations, with rows for native-parity, wrapper-patched, intentionally-delegated, raw-known-gap, and owner-acknowledged limitation; wire the coverage doc to this ledger so future review rounds do not rediscover or miss the same divergence class.
close_reason: Shipped scripts/upstream_divergence_ledger.py + docs/upstream_divergence_ledger.{md,json}. Combines AST classification (delegation_ledger.json), per-fixture audit (raw_vs_public_audit.json), Rust KNOWN GAP comment scan, and closed-bead history into one ledger. 542 entries across 536 functions: 382 native-parity, 153 intentionally-delegated, 5 wrapper-patched, 1 raw-known-gap (_raw_is_planar), 1 owner-acknowledged-limitation. 5 regression tests in tests/python/test_upstream_divergence_ledger.py lock in the surface. title: is_dominating_set rejects directed input; nx supports it

title: node identifiers: fnx conflates int N with str(N) — diverges from nx Python-dict semantics
description: fnx.Graph.add_edge(1, '1') silently creates ONE node and ONE self-loop edge (1, 1), but nx creates TWO nodes (1, '1') and one edge between them. Root cause in crates/fnx-python/src/lib.rs:89 'node_key_to_string': canonicalizes int 1 → '1' (via i64.to_string()), same canonical as string '1'. This intentionally collapses True/False/0/0.0 to match Python's hash(True)==hash(1), but extends incorrectly to int-string collisions where Python dict treats them as distinct. Affects: G.add_edge(1, '1'), G.add_node(1) then G.add_node('1'), and any code that mixes int and string node IDs. Fix is architectural (per-node Python-type tracking), so P2 + large scope.
close_reason: done

title: reverse_view dispatch silently bypassed — sister of br-r37-c1-gshwt
description:
Continuing the view-dispatch audit: _ReverseDirectedView and _ReverseMultiDirectedView subclass DiGraph/MultiDiGraph respectively. They isinstance-pass the second check in _coerce_arg_to_fnx_graph (which returns the graph as-is for fnx-typed inputs) — but their Rust inner adjacency still holds the ORIGINAL edges. The view's Python-side reversal is invisible to the Rust kernel.

Verified bug: fnx.shortest_path_length(reverse_view(0→1→2→3→4), 4, 0) raised NetworkXNoPath because the Rust kernel saw the forward edges 0→1→...→4 and couldn't find any backward path. nx returns 4.

_raw_single_source_shortest_path_length(fr, 4) returned {4: 0} — confirming the Rust kernel reads through the reverse view.

Fix: add isinstance check for _ReverseDirectedViewBase BEFORE the (Graph, DiGraph, MultiGraph, MultiDiGraph) isinstance check in _coerce_arg_to_fnx_graph. Materialize via _from_nx_graph (which deserializes the reverse view's edges() into a fresh fnx DiGraph).

Verified: shortest_path_length, topological_sort, pagerank now all match nx exactly on reverse_view inputs. 17516 tests pass (17515 → 17516 with the regression-lock test).
notes: Fixed in same commit by adding _ReverseDirectedViewBase isinstance check to _coerce_arg_to_fnx_graph.

title: HITS: svds(k=1) returns mixed-sign vector on bipartite graphs -> test_hits_structural_invariants[weighted-path-5] fails
description:
## Symptom
`tests/python/test_centrality_conformance_matrix.py::test_hits_structural_invariants[weighted-path-5]` currently FAILS:

    fnx hubs = {0: 0.9999999999999996, 1: -1.4999999999999991, 2: 1.9999999999999991, 3: -1.4999999999999993, 4: 0.9999999999999998}
    nx  hubs = {0: 0.0168, 1: 0.114, 2: 0.215, 3: 0.412, 4: 0.242}

fnx returns MIXED-SIGN hub/authority scores. HITS scores must be non-negative (principal/Perron singular vector). The sign-tolerant assertion added in 1452a7f07 only accepts a GLOBAL sign flip (all negated); fnx's vector is a different vector in the degenerate singular subspace, so it is neither v nor -v and the check fails.

## Root cause
`python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:hits()` mirrors NetworkX: `vt = scipy.sparse.linalg.svds(A, k=1, v0=nstart, ...)`. For BIPARTITE graphs (path_graph is bipartite) the adjacency matrix's largest singular value is degenerate (eigenvalues come in +/-lambda pairs, so |+lambda| = |-lambda| = sigma_max with multiplicity 2). svds(k=1) then returns an arbitrary vector from that 2-D degenerate subspace, which can be mixed-sign. With v0=None ARPACK uses a random start, so the result is non-reproducible run-to-run AND between fnx/nx. NetworkX has the same theoretical issue but happened to land on the clean Perron vector here.

## Not caused by current review-session work
Independent of the degree_centrality (d07503f74) and MST tie-break (cb7ae4bed) fixes — HITS uses a separate svds path. Related history: br-hitsdelta, br-karateorder, br-r37-c1-tiuam (the docstring + sign-tolerant test already acknowledge svds basis/sign ambiguity).

## Suggested fix directions (need design decision)
1. For undirected/symmetric A, compute HITS via the deterministic non-negative power iteration that ALREADY exists in Rust (`fnx_algorithms::hits_centrality`, lib.rs ~2554) instead of svds — converges to the Perron vector, always non-negative and reproducible. Trade-off: may not byte-match nx's svds output on non-degenerate graphs.
2. Post-process the svds vector: project onto non-negative orthant / take `abs` of the dominant-sign convention and re-run one A^T A multiply to land on Perron. 
3. Pin a deterministic v0 and document that exact nx parity is impossible on bipartite/degenerate spectra (widen the test to assert HITS structural invariants — non-negativity, normalization, symmetry — rather than elementwise nx equality).
close_reason: Fixed weighted matrix exports to honor Python-visible edge weight mutations; HITS weighted-path regression passes

title: perf: _fnx_to_nx MULTIGRAPH conversion is pathologically slow (no native bulk reader) — hits all multigraph delegations
description: backend.py _fnx_to_nx takes the SLOW AtlasView path for multigraphs: _topo_emit_edges_by_adj + per-edge fg[u][v].items() + _align_rows re-walk fg.adj per node. cProfile (200-node MultiGraph) = ~540ms, 98% conversion. Simple graphs have _native_fnx_to_nx_adjacency bulk reader; multigraphs do NOT. Add a native bulk multigraph adjacency reader (parallel keys+attrs in adj-insertion order) so ALL multigraph delegations speed up (MD:scc 11.76x, MG/MD pagerank 1.5x, any nx-delegated multigraph algo). Risk: must preserve edges(keys=True) order for adj-dependent delegated algos.
notes: PARTIAL FIX shipped 3eec03000: UNDIRECTED multigraph _fnx_to_nx now uses fast native bulk edge build + adjacency() row align — conversion 2584ms->8.9ms (290x), byte-exact (fingerprint aa6c391f), degree_assortativity(weight=) 2594ms->51ms. REMAINING (directed): MultiDiGraph still on slow path because _pred rows need fg's edge-insertion pred order, which has no cheap Python source — needs a native predecessor-keys bulk reader (Rust). That unblocks MD:wcc 126x, MD:scc 19x, MD:attracting 9x.
close_reason: FULLY FIXED. Undirected 3eec03000 (290x). Directed 0afef75bf: added native Rust PyMultiDiGraph::_native_predecessor_keys_bulk ({node:[preds in fg.pred order]}) so backend.py realigns _pred without the O(V*deg) AtlasView walk. Conversion fingerprint byte-exact NEW==OLD (undir aa6c391f, dir 310a2021); 10508 multi/convert/conformance + 10 row-parity tests pass. _fnx_to_nx(MD) n=500 12351ms->28.9ms (427x); degree_assortativity(weight=) MD 2384ms->89ms (26.7x); complement 4x. Every delegated multigraph fn that routes through _fnx_to_nx inherits it.

title: Centrality goldens test brittle to last-digit ULP drift (.15g precision)
description: test_standard_graph_centrality_goldens.py uses NUMERIC_FORMAT='.15g' (15 significant digits) and asserts byte-exact JSON equality. At 15 sig digits the comparison sits at the float64 precision boundary, so equally-correct implementations differ by 1 ULP in the LAST digit due to BLAS/CPU/optimisation variance. Current failures: davis_southern_women eigenvector_centrality['Brenda Rogers'] 0.221330624547406 vs golden 0.221330624547407, florentine_families pagerank['Tornabuoni'] 0.0712796858189296 vs golden 0.0712796858189295 — both are last-digit ULPs. Test intent is regression lock for centrality correctness, not bit-exact reproduction. Fix: compare values numerically with math.isclose(rel_tol=1e-12) — locks 12+ sig digits (well above any meaningful drift) while tolerating ULP noise; still detects any algorithmic regression.
close_reason: Switched to math.isclose(rel_tol=1e-12) for score values, strict equality on structural metadata (commit just landed). Test passes.

title: perf: edges(nbunch,data) AtlasView walk 13x slower than nx — route to to_dict_of_dicts rows
description: EdgeDataView._materialize_via_adj_walk (G.edges(nbunch,data=...)) did per-node graph[node].items() AtlasView + per-edge dict() copy (~13x slower than nx). For large-enough nbunch (>=1/4 node_count) snapshot the whole adjacency via native to_dict_of_dicts ({node:{nbr:live_dict}}, plain-dict rows) and read its rows; small nbunch keeps the AtlasView walk (now no dict() copy). 6.7-7.3x self-speedup, 13x->1.8x slower vs nx; data=True now yields nx's LIVE dict (was a copy). byte-exact 250 cases vs nx, golden 5dcd6a75. RESIDUAL: full-graph build overbuilds for partial nbunch -> faster-than-nx needs a native edges(nbunch,data) kernel walking only nbunch's rows w/ live-dict attach.
notes: NATIVE-KERNEL TEMPLATE (for faster-than-nx): mirror to_dict_of_dicts_undirected (fnx-python/src/.rs) but (1) iterate ONLY nbunch nodes in given order, (2) undirected seen-dedup (skip nbr already a processed source; add node to seen AFTER inner loop to keep self-loops), (3) flatten to Vec<(PyObject,PyObject,PyObject)> = (py_u, py_v, edge_dict) where edge_dict = pg.edge_py_attrs.get(&PyGraph::edge_key(u,v)).map(bind).unwrap_or_else(|| PyDict::new). data=False -> 2-tuples; data=str -> edge_dict.get(name,default). Register + route EdgeDataView._materialize_via_adj_walk to it (undirected Graph only). This walks only nbunch rows (no full-graph to_dict_of_dicts overbuild) -> beats nx. Regression risk: hot edges() path; gate type(graph) is Graph, comprehensive parity vs nx incl self-loops/data variants.
close_reason: shipped native edges_nbunch_data + edges_nbunch_count kernels: walks only nbunch (no overbuild) + kills __len__ double-materialize. attr-present 0.63x->1.07x FASTER than nx, attrless 0.54x->0.96x parity, 1.7-1.8x self-speedup over prior commit (~12x from original), byte-identical golden 5dcd6a75, 1297 tests pass. title: perf: enumerate_all_cliques algorithm — track common-neighbour candidate set incrementally (nx-style) instead of re-checking all clique members per candidate
description: After the index-space binding (d83cc22b0) enumerate_all_cliques is still ~1.5x slower than nx at n=300 because the kernel (fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs enumerate_all_cliques_index ~27058) does clique.iter().all(|&c| adj[c].contains(&candidate)) for every candidate in (last+1)..n — O(clique_size * n) per clique. nx's enumerate_all_cliques carries a per-clique CANDIDATE set (common neighbours so far) and only iterates that, O(candidates). Rewrite to thread a sorted common-neighbour Vec/bitset per clique down the BFS levels; candidates for extending clique+v = (current candidates) ∩ N(v) with index>last. Byte-exact: same emission order (single nodes in node order, then ascending candidates per level). Bench n=300 deg6 target <=1.0x nx.
close_reason: Fixed 8d3f8573d: replaced the O(|clique|·n) per-clique candidate re-scan with nx's incremental candidate-set BFS — each queue entry carries (clique, ascending higher-index common neighbours); extending with cnbrs[i] yields cnbrs[i+1..] ∩ N(cnbrs[i]). n=300 deg6 1.97ms (1.51x nx) -> 0.33ms (0.25x = 4x faster), 6x self-speedup; 0.24-0.26x nx across n=100..400. Golden sha byte-identical to prior kernel (360f85f0…); byte-exact vs nx 60 graphs; 2 Rust + 366 Python tests pass.

notes: Landed in 0eb3684c2. Rust exposes monotonic u64 nodes_seq + edges_seq counters on all 4 PyGraph classes via #[getter]. title: dag_longest_path_length ignores weights from add_weighted_edges_from / post-mutation
description: fnx.dag_longest_path_length(G, weight='weight') returns the hop count instead of the summed edge weight when edge weights were set via add_weighted_edges_from or post-creation mutation G[u][v]['weight']=w. Repro: D=fnx.DiGraph(); D.add_weighted_edges_from([(0,1,5),(1,2,3)]); fnx.dag_longest_path_length(D,weight='weight') == 2 (nx: 8). On a branching DAG it returns 2 vs nx 20. ROOT CAUSE: the wrapper trusts the Rust _fnx.dag_longest_path_length fast path, gated by _dag_length_rust_weight_attrs_safe which only validates the Python-visible value type — but the Rust edge_attrs() layer cannot see weights stored by add_weighted_edges_from / post-mutation (Rust attr-sync gap), so directed_edge_weight_with_default falls back to default_weight for every edge. The dag_longest_path wrapper is unaffected (pure-Python, reads G.pred). FIX: compute dag_longest_path_length from the correct pure-Python dag_longest_path + sum weights via Python G[u][v] access, mirroring nx exactly; drop the broken Rust fast path. Surfaced by test_review_mode_regression_lock.py::test_dag_longest_path_length_preserves_int_type_match_nx.
close_reason: Rewrote dag_longest_path_length to compute from the pure-Python weight-correct dag_longest_path, summing weights via Python G[u][v] access (nx's exact algorithm, multigraph branch included). Dropped the broken Rust _fnx.dag_longest_path_length fast path and both now-dead gate helpers. Commit 5aff1dc0. Verified vs nx across 8 graph shapes; 99 dag tests pass including the regression-lock test. Underlying Rust attr-sync gap remains tracked as br-r37-c1-7s20o.

description: Discovered by br-r37-c1-256q5 delegation ledger runtime probes (docs/delegation_ledger.md). All three shapes tested (path-5-unweighted, path-5-weighted-kwargs, path-5-weighted-postmut) classify as nx-fallback. The Rust _raw_floyd_warshall binding exists and is exposed. Investigate: why does the wrapper unconditionally delegate? If it's because of historical correctness concerns, the sjf4t-style sync fix may now eliminate them, allowing the native path. Verify against tests/python/test_floyd_warshall_avg_neighbor_order_parity.py and the floyd_warshall family in test_*shortest_path*.py before flipping.
close_reason: Investigated: the wrapper unconditionally delegates because of br-r37-c1-h1kf2 (nx's defaultdict-based iteration order is algorithm-dependent and matching it from Rust wasn't deemed worth the perf gain — the comment notes 'Rust impl is faster only marginally on typically-small-N workload'). Deliberate design decision, not a missed opportunity. Closing as wontfix; if profiling later shows floyd_warshall is hot, revisit by re-implementing iteration in Rust to match nx exactly.

title: View materialization cache silently returned stale results on count-preserving rewires (revert of br-r37-c1-a2rxy)
description:
Last turn's br-r37-c1-a2rxy added a (node_count, edge_count) cache key to _materialize_filtered_view to speed up repeated algorithm calls on subgraph_view / reverse_view inputs. The cache produced correct results on add_node, remove_node, add_edge, remove_edge in isolation — but silently returned STALE materializations on COUNT-PRESERVING rewires:

  fg = fnx.Graph(); fg.add_edges_from([(0,1),(1,2),(2,3)])  # path
  fv = fnx.subgraph_view(fg, filter_node=lambda n: True)
  fnx.shortest_path_length(fv, 0, 3)  # = 3 (warms cache key (4,3))
  
  fg.remove_edge(1, 2); fg.add_edge(0, 3)  # rewire to direct shortcut
  fnx.shortest_path_length(fv, 0, 3)  # returns 3 from cache; nx returns 1

Revert the cache (correctness > perf).  Locked the no-cache behaviour via test_view_materialization_invalidates_on_count_preserving_rewire.

Filed br-r37-c1-mutseq as the proper fix: expose a Rust-side monotonic mutation counter (nodes_seq + new edges_seq) to Python so the cache key can be tuple(seq) and ANY mutation invalidates correctly.
notes: Reverted in same commit; regression test landed.

title: HITS non-deterministic on degenerate-singular-value graphs (matches nx algorithm, inherits nx random-v0 ambiguity)
description: NOT A BUG / known acceptable divergence. fnx.hits is byte-for-byte nx algorithm (svds(A,k=1,v0=nstart) then A@a). On NON-degenerate inputs (simple dominant singular value) fnx matches nx at machine precision (verified 1.7e-17, 0.0 across seeds). When A top singular value is DEGENERATE (multiplicity>1 — disconnected DiGraph/MultiDiGraph and some small symmetric structures), svds returns an arbitrary vector from the top singular SUBSPACE chosen by ARPACK random v0, so BOTH fnx AND nx are non-deterministic and emit negative/sign-flipped components varying run-to-run with global numpy RNG (verified nx.hits gives different values on repeated calls). Documented in fnx.hits docstring (br-r37-c1-hitsdegen). Do NOT force a deterministic vector (would diverge from nx random-v0 contract). Future gauntlet/probes should skip HITS on degenerate graphs. Found via directed/multi gauntlet seed 213.
close_reason: DUPLICATE of already-closed br-r37-c1-a0466 (HITS degenerate-singular-value non-determinism). Already documented in memory project_intentional_divergences.md and locked by tests/python/test_hits_wellconditioned_parity.py. Re-discovered via gauntlet before checking the intentional-divergences ledger. The docstring note (283ce8408, br-r37-c1-hitsdegen) is still a net positive (inline doc was previously only the karate note) but the bead is redundant.

title: [perf][no-gaps] native Rust bidirectional_dijkstra kernel to beat nx (~7x gap remains)
description: After 6471564eb the in-process Python bidirectional_dijkstra is byte-exact + 14x faster than the old delegation, but still ~0.14x (7x slower) than nx: the fnx Python adjacency-view access (G.adj[v].items()) in the heap loop is the residual tax. To BEAT nx, write a native Rust kernel in fnx-algorithms (integer-CSR adjacency + BinaryHeap, replicate nx's shared-counter tie-break + neighbour order for byte-exact path). Same lever class as global_efficiency CSR (f05e53e60). Target: faster than nx (0.14ms@n=2000 -> sub-0.1ms). Parity proof = the 600-case golden (sha d5590b0f...). Same residual affects astar/negative_edge_cycle/single_source_dijkstra single-pair (all fnx-adjacency-tax bound) - candidates for native single-pair path kernels.
close_reason: Native undirected CSR bidirectional_dijkstra kernel landed 09decd754: ports _bidirectional_dijkstra_local 1:1 to all-Rust (integer CSR adjacency, shared FIFO counter, byte-exact path + int/float type via inner CgseValue). 480-case native==local==nx (length+type+path) 0 mism, golden sha 86dca111, full suite 21277 passed. 1.2-6.5x faster than the prior in-process port, beats nx at n<=500. Residual large-n gap = dispatcher O(E) weight pre-scan, split to br-r37-c1-6nzsy. DiGraph keeps in-process port (no in-neighbour CSR accessor).

title: [bug][parity] single-pair shortest_path/bidirectional_shortest_path + target-only branch: discovery objects for bidirectional/reverse walks (6hpa9 final residual)
description: Last residual of the discovery-object family (10/11 fns + the forward path-dict family fixed across d7cbf073d/866bf273a/03cac6c00/f5b6cca43). REMAINING: (a) shortest_path(G, s, t) + bidirectional_shortest_path — nx runs bidirectional BFS: nodes discovered by the FORWARD frontier carry succ-row objects, BACKWARD frontier pred-row objects, and the path stitches at the meet node; replicating needs the kernel to emit (node, parent, side) or the binding to re-run nx's exact two-frontier walk; (b) shortest_path(G, target=t) directed branch loops per-node single-pair (also slow, O(V) bidirectional calls) — should use one reverse-BFS single_target walk with pred-row discovery + its key order. Probe: g.add_node(28); g.add_edge(7, 28.0); g.add_edge(28.0, 5); shortest_path(g,7,5) -> nx ['7','28.0','5'] vs fnx ['7','28','5'].
notes: MAIN FIX SHIPPED c8512ed5a: bidirectional meta kernels (value tie-break parity + discovery objects), directed handled natively (delegation + conversion tax removed). REMAINING for close: the target-only shortest_path(G, target=t) branch — needs a single reverse-walk single_target kernel (key order = reverse-BFS discovery from t, objects = pred-row discovery, replaces the O(V) per-node bidirectional loop). Probe: sp(target=5) key order nx starts with target; fnx starts elsewhere + node-map objects.
close_reason: Fully closed across c8512ed5a (bidirectional meta kernels: tie-break VALUE parity + discovery objects, directed delegation removed) + 3c69015f6 (target-only = nx single_target reverse BFS: key order + tie-breaks + objects, O(V) bidirectional loop -> one walk, 4.6ms@3k). The whole 6hpa9 discovery-object family probe is now 0-divergence.

Includes direct guard for the original weighted-degree-double-count bug. 19 cases + 1 skip.

title: fix(dijkstra): emit shortest-path dicts in networkx finalize (heap-pop) order
description: single_source_dijkstra_path/_length and all_pairs_dijkstra* return Rust HashMaps, so the Python dict key order is hash-order, not nx's. nx orders these dicts by node-finalization order (heap pop = sort by (distance, push-seq)). Probe: key-order mismatch dijkstra 34/236, bellman 157/236 on random weighted graphs (BFS single_source_shortest_path already correct 0/236 via ordered Vec return). Fix: record finalize order in multi_source_dijkstra core; convert dijkstra leaf+all_pairs fns to order-preserving Vec<(String,V)> like single_source_shortest_path; bindings build ordered PyDict. Bellman order rule (SPFA discovery) tracked separately.
close_reason: Rust dijkstra kernel now emits entries in nx finalize (heap-pop) order; single_source_dijkstra_full/_directed + single_source_dijkstra_directed + all_pairs_dijkstra* return ordered Vec<(node,val)>. Python wrappers trust kernel order (stable sort by distance) instead of BFS-hop tiebreak. Verified 0 key-order mismatches across 1612 single-source + 400 all_pairs instances (directed+undirected), value parity intact; 132 new regression tests + full 19150-test suite pass.

title: [code-review] pagerank fast path ignores max_iter non-convergence
description: [HIGH] fnx.pagerank uses the Rust fast path for simple non-negative graphs but pagerank_with_weight always returns a PageRankResult after the iteration loop, even when no convergence criterion was met. Repro: fnx.pagerank(fnx.path_graph(3), max_iter=0) returns uniform scores, while networkx.pagerank(nx.path_graph(3), max_iter=0) raises PowerIterationFailedConvergence. Similarly a directed graph with max_iter=1 and tol=1e-20 returns partial scores in fnx but raises in NetworkX. Fix should propagate convergence state from the Rust PageRank implementation to the PyO3 wrapper and raise PowerIterationFailedConvergence(max_iter) on exhaustion.
close_reason: Fixed convergence check

Same nx-on-fnx-substrate slowness likely affects: clustering, redundancy, spectral_bipartivity, node_redundancy, robins_alexander_clustering, betweenness/closeness/degree centrality (bipartite), etc. Audit each with fnx.bipartite.X(F) vs nx; for whole-graph ones add a one-shot _matching_nx_view-style override (lightweight convert + delegate). bipartite.clustering was 0.88x (borderline).

Remaining bipartite fns assessed: density+degree_centrality already overridden (bipdense). clustering/average_clustering/robins_alexander/node_redundancy run nx's exact float-sum over a CPython set (order-dependent) -> bit-exact ONLY by reusing nx's code; a one-shot nx-view convert measured ~0.94-0.99x (reaches parity, never beats nx) so not worth the conversion + would not change exactness. betweenness/spectral_bipartivity/to_vertex_cover are algorithm-dominated (~0.98-1.0x, dispatch amortized). closeness was the one clean Score>=2.0 win.

title: [bug] nested directed subgraph degree can raise KeyError via _fast_pred_row
description: During br-r37-c1-r3gjb nested edge proof setup, adding view.degree() to a nested DiGraph/MultiDiGraph subgraph snapshot raised KeyError from _fast_pred_row(parent, node) inside _AssignedPrivateDegreeView._filtered_set_count. Repro shape: build directed graph, take G.subgraph(keep_outer).subgraph(keep_inner), then iterate view.degree(); current edge-view proof excluded degree to keep the perf lever scoped. Fix separately: nested filtered degree fast path must either collapse to a concrete parent with composed filters or fall back when parent is another _FilteredGraphView.
close_reason: FIXED. Root cause: _fast_pred_row/_fast_succ_row/_fast_adj_row called the native _native_*_row_dict via getattr, but a filtered/reverse VIEW INHERITS those methods from its concrete base class while having an EMPTY Rust base -> native_dict(node) raised KeyError for a nested subgraph's view parent (in/total degree on nested DiGraph/MultiDiGraph). Fix: gate native-row usage on type(graph) in concrete (Di/Multi)Graph types; views fall through to graph.pred[node]/graph[node] (filter-aware). Also gated _filtered_set_count on a concrete parent. Nested subgraph degree (total/weighted/in/out/single-node, incl triple-nested) bit-exact 0/120, NO KeyError; full pytest 22104 passed. Separate PRE-EXISTING nested-edges ORDER divergence filed as new bead (not part of this KeyError bug).

title: nx.set_{node,edge}_attributes(fnx_graph, ...) fails — dispatcher rejects fnx graph for mutation-preserving
description: nx.set_node_attributes(fnx_graph, {...}, name='color') and nx.set_edge_attributes(fnx_graph, {...}, name='weight') both raise NotImplementedError: Backend 'franken_networkx' does not implement '...'. These are mutation-preserving functions (they mutate the input graph) so the nx dispatcher refuses to auto-convert. fnx already has fnx.set_node_attributes and fnx.set_edge_attributes — just need to register them in franken_networkx.backend._SUPPORTED_ALGORITHMS so the dispatcher routes through fnx's implementation. Same fix pattern as br-r37-c1-0epvo for tree-submodule. Likely affects every common workflow that loads attribute data into a fnx graph via the nx API.
close_reason: done

title: perf+fix(onion_layers): native kernel matching nx (current_core high-water mark + stable order) — drop delegation
description: onion_layers DELEGATES to nx (the Rust kernel diverged on karate), so it pays the full _fnx_to_nx conversion: 7x slower than nx (3000n: 0.049s vs 0.007s, ~0.043s of it conversion). Root cause of divergence: nx peels ALL nodes with degree<=current_core (a high-water mark of min degree) per layer; the Rust kernel only removed degree==min_deg nodes, splitting them into extra layers. FIX the kernel: current_core high-water mark + this_layer=all alive deg<=current_core, sorted by (degree, insertion-idx) for nx's stable-sort key order, isolated nodes emitted as layer 1 first. Then route the Python wrapper to native (no delegation). Prototype matches nx values 401/401 (incl karate) and key order 400/400. Expect ~10x (delegated 0.049s -> native ~0.005s).
close_reason: Fixed native onion_layers kernel to match nx exactly (current_core high-water-mark peeling of all deg<=current_core, this_layer sorted by (degree,insertion-idx), isolated nodes as layer 1); routed Python wrapper to native (onion_layers_rust) instead of delegating to nx. Before->after: onion_layers 3000n 0.049s->0.0040s (~12x faster); now 0.58x of nx (FASTER than nx, was 7x slower). Isomorphism: 0 value + 0 key-order mismatch across 501 graphs incl karate/isolated/subgraph-view/nx-typed; karate golden sha256==nx; error contracts (directed/multigraph/selfloop) all raise NetworkXNotImplemented. 66 new tests + full 20558-test suite pass; clippy clean.

NEW keyed bulk APIs for MultiDiGraph AND MultiGraph + resolve-aware shadow merge); lazy mirrors; fused disjoint_union; native compose serving union; native disjointness check. title: is_semieulerian directly imports _fnx kernel that rejects directed input — nx supports DiGraph

title: [perf] view iteration per-element PyO3 tax: list(nodes) 28x->18.5x (clone-Vec removed), residual = per-next dispatch + String-hash lookup; adjacency() 63x, neighbors 13x OPEN
description: Multi-shape scan (large sparse/star) found view-iteration gaps: list(G.nodes()) 28x, list(adjacency()) 63x, list(neighbors(hub)) 13x, list(G[hub]) 11.5x — nx serves these as C-speed dict/lazy-view iteration. SHIPPED: NodeView NoData fast path drops the throwaway Vec<String> clone (28x->18.5x). RESIDUAL (this bead): (1) per-element NodeViewIterator.__next__ Python->Rust dispatch x N + (2) node_key_map String-hash per node — both need index-ordered display-object storage (Vec<PyObject> parallel to nodes IndexMap) to clone_ref directly without hashing, OR a native-list-iterator that keeps the staleness check. adjacency()/neighbors share the per-element tax. NOTE: list(edges) already ~parity (1.37x).
notes: DECISIVE MEASUREMENT (load 8): list(nodes) cost is per-__next__ DISPATCH, not String-hash. for-loop-consume (dispatch only) 2.198ms ~= list(nodes) 2.053ms; iter()-construct (the __iter__ Vec build incl py_node_key hashing) ~0.001ms. => the index-aligned display-object Vec would NOT help (hash isn't the bottleneck). The residual IS the custom staleness-checking NodeViewIterator's per-element PyO3 __next__ (~110ns x N); nx gets C-speed+staleness free from dict_keyiterator. Eliminating it requires a C-level Python list_iterator, which DROPS the mutation-during-iteration RuntimeError contract (parity-absolute). DEAD-END for the no-loss path; reclassify P3. adjacency() 63x = fnx has no persistent Python _adj (fundamental). DO NOT build the index-aligned object Vec for this.
close_reason: Completed node-view no-data lever: removed obsolete Python mutation wrapper after baseline profile. Golden SHA unchanged f4f9df319125a62746af325ffaba898359ad2470b9a2edd6de0abb2ba84f08c4; direct medians list(G.nodes) 1.8206ms -> 0.7008ms (2.60x), consume 2.0378ms -> 1.0030ms (2.03x); hyperfine process loop 370.1ms -> 316.9ms (1.17x incl import/setup); focused pytest 4 passed. Follow-up adjacency/neighbors mirror filed as br-r37-c1-p34di.

title: perf(k_components): native Moody-White to drop fnx->nx delegation+conversion (target: parity-or-faster vs genuine nx)
description: k_components delegates to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (fnx->nx conversion + nx Moody-White recursion). Genuine nx k_components is itself very slow on dense/high-connectivity graphs (node_connectivity maxflows on many subgraphs). Native integer-CSR Moody-White (max k-connected subgraphs via (k-1)-connected substructures, reusing fnx's fast node_connectivity/edge-connectivity kernels) would remove both the delegation conversion AND beat nx. title: to_scipy_sparse_array: native Rust COO builder (follow-up to br-r37-c1-exj4e)
description: Closing the residual 9-12x perf gap on to_scipy_sparse_array / to_numpy_array vs nx requires a Rust-native helper that builds the (rows, cols, data) numpy arrays in one PyO3 call, eliminating per-edge Python tuple alloc. Estimate: 100-200 LOC across fnx-python + a numpy dependency in the binding. Not blocking but a clear performance win for hits, katz_centrality_numpy, laplacian_matrix, effective_graph_resistance, and any other matrix-based algorithm.
notes:
Implemented native Rust adjacency_arrays helper in crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs. Iterates fnx_classes::Graph::edges_ordered_borrowed() to build (rows, cols, data) Vecs in a single PyO3 call. Wired into to_scipy_sparse_array and to_numpy_array for non-multigraph fnx Graph/DiGraph inputs. Maintains nx int-dtype contract via a Python-side post-check (all data values integer-valued => int64 coerce).

Measured on 200-node W-S graph:
  to_scipy_sparse_array:  ~9.9ms -> ~2.6ms (~4x faster)
  to_numpy_array:         ~4.3ms -> ~2.6ms (~1.7x faster)
  laplacian_matrix:       ~8.8ms -> ~3.3ms (~2.6x faster)

17506 Python tests pass.

title: [perf] communicability_betweenness_centrality 1.7x slower + 35s O(n^4) — alien-artifact target
description: Profile-backed: communicability_betweenness_centrality on 300-edge graph = 35.7s vs nx 21.6s = 1.7x SLOWER. Algorithm is O(n^4): for each node, zero its row/col in the adjacency and recompute a dense matrix exponential (expm via eigh, O(n^3)) — n times. nx does the same O(n^4) so even parity is 21s. DEEP TARGET (no-gaps/alien-artifact mandate): needs a fundamentally better algorithm, not a faster loop — e.g. avoid recomputing expm from scratch per node-removal (low-rank/Krylov update of the matrix exponential under row/col zeroing), or a scaling-and-squaring expm with shared factors across removals. Multi-session numerical research task. Also has an open self-loop edge-case (memory). Target: beat nx (sub-21s) + parity.
close_reason: communicability_betweenness_centrality 1.7x SLOWER -> 12.4x FASTER than nx (n=200: 2.6s vs 32.5s). The n per-node passes (each a full O(n^3) symmetric matrix-exp of A with row/col r zeroed) are INDEPENDENT; parallelized across cores with std::thread::scope (safe Rust, no new dep; PyO3 binding already released the GIL). nx's reference is single-threaded O(n^4) Python. Bit-IDENTICAL output (same per-node arithmetic): 0/40 golden parity across n=4-60 spanning the n>=24 parallel threshold; 1241 communicability/centrality tests + full pytest 22100 pass. Sequential fallback for n<24 (thread setup would dominate). NOTE: still O(n^4) WORK — a true O(n^3) algorithm (low-rank/Krylov expm update under row/col deletion) remains an open alien-artifact target if the constant ever matters; parallelism was the shippable lever here. 64-core box.

title: [perf] watts_strogatz_graph O(E^2) rewire loop — per-rewire Graph.remove_edge is O(E) (shift_remove_full); 19.4x at p=0.5
description:
Profile-backed: watts_strogatz_graph(5000,6,p) fnx vs nx: p=0 1.74x (build), p=0.1 6.17x, p=0.5 19.42x — cost scales with rewire probability. cProfile: 100% in native _fnx.watts_strogatz_graph. Root cause: watts_strogatz_graph_core (crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs:6020) rewire loop calls Graph.remove_edge per rewire; fnx-classes remove_edge (lib.rs) uses self.edges.shift_remove_full (O(E) IndexMap shift) -> loop is O(E^2). nx's dict-del rewire is O(1)/rewire. Also hits barabasi_albert 2.39x and likely double_edge_swap / any many-remove_edge algo. FIX OPTIONS: (a) local rewire in the core — Vec> adjacency rows + HashSet<(usize,usize)> membership + Vec degree, replicate EXACT rng-draw order (rng.random()

=n-1 skip) AND the remove-then-append adjacency-row order, then rebuild Graph from final rows row-order-preserving; (b) remove_edge shift_remove->swap_remove (O(1) but reorders edges IndexMap — verify nothing relies on edges-map order; broad/risky, affects all callers). CONSTRAINT: byte-identical seeded output — golden sha across seeds x p in {0,0.1,0.5} + adjacency iteration order vs nx. Needs Rust build + golden proof; not an in-the-hour change.
close_reason: watts_strogatz_graph O(E^2) rewire FIXED: local adjacency-row rewire (retain O(deg) + HashSet O(1) membership, exact rng-draw order) + apply_row_orders to reproduce the remove-then-append adjacency order, then build once. p=0.5 19.42x->1.42x (385ms->24ms), p=0.1 6.17x->2.01x. Byte-identical to nx across 8 seeds x 4 (n,k) x 5 p incl odd-k/k==n edge cases (golden 460e4b62); full pytest 22040. fnx-generators only. NOTE: remove_edge O(E) (shift_remove_full) still hits double_edge_swap + other many-removal algos — separate bead-worthy.

description: MultiGraph AdjacencyView re-materialization catastrophe sweep (the G.adj[node]-in-node-loop class). Already fixed: non_neighbors(3768b162f), selfloop_edges(7132af442), edge_bfs/edge_dfs(a24ed2afd, 6u0wl), adjacency_data. STILL SLOW on MultiGraph (measured via fnx.X median, N=1200/m=6000): to_dict_of_dicts 45x, dfs_tree 10x, to_dict_of_lists 4.8x, bfs_edges 17x (native _bfs_edges_raw path — different, not the re-materialization trap). RECIPE for the re-materialization ones: nbrdict=G.adj[node] -> G[node] (native O(deg) row, G[node].items() multi keydict); G.pred[node] -> G._native_predecessor_row_dict(node). G[node]/native-row order == AdjacencyView order (verified 0/300). to_dict_of_dicts: native _fnx kernel is simple-only, multigraph falls to G.adj[node] loop -> fix or add multi native kernel. bfs_edges 17x is a SEPARATE native-BFS-multi perf issue (not adj[node] trap). AUDIT remaining `.adj[`/`.succ[`/`.pred[`-in-loop callers: 18145 _pagerank_outgoing_weights, 20453 _bellman_ford_adjacency_entries, 6302 _networkx_graph_for_traversal_parity, 13600.
notes: to_dict_of_dicts MultiGraph residual (38x) is ARCHITECTURALLY FLOORED, not a quick lever: nx reuses persistent keydict objects (d[u][v] IS G[u][v], write-through) -> 0.24ms; fnx has NO persistent Python keydict across the Rust boundary, so it must construct a _LiveMultiEdgeDataView (read-only, deliberate lmev-mutate design + tests) PER EDGE (~6.7ms floor). Plain-dict return would change the tested read-only contract (footgun: silent non-write-through mutation); native pyclass view breaks isinstance(_,dict). Real fix = persistent per-(u,v) keydict cache in PyMultiGraph substrate (like edge_py_attrs caches datadicts) — large. DFS/BFS/to_dict_of_lists/to_scipy/pagerank multigraph all DONE this arc. Consider closing mexh6 and tracking to_dict_of_dicts substrate separately.
close_reason: Kept slot-allocation lever for _LiveMultiEdgeDataView: direct MultiGraph 0.850623s -> 0.474197s, MultiDiGraph 0.384603s -> 0.262788s; proof SHA 1cef3a7ef93d10e2a178b103bf5a0f26108a7f345d5c6a8b5f7bdaaba3dfd505. Residual persistent keydict substrate routed to br-r37-c1-rup1h.

title: Seeded functions reject random.Random instance, nx accepts
description: fnx.newman_watts_strogatz_graph / connected_watts_strogatz_graph / maximal_independent_set raise TypeError when passed seed=random.Random(42); nx accepts random.Random via @py_random_state decorator. The Rust binding requires int seed; the Python wrappers (or the shared _native_random_seed helper) do not convert random.Random instances to int. Fix: extend _native_random_seed to extract a u64 from a random.Random via seed.randrange(1<<64), and ensure maximal_independent_set uses it (currently has its own inline normalization that also misses random.Random).
close_reason: _native_random_seed now handles random.Random via .randrange(1<<64); maximal_independent_set routed through it. 3 seed-types mismatches -> 0; 64 tests pass.

title: perf: route remaining nx-aliased algorithm submodules (components/dag/clique/cuts/distance_measures/structuralholes/flow/link_analysis) to fnx top-level
description: Same pattern as br-r37-c1-1s6cb (centrality, shipped 750bb2714, 32x). These submodules are still aliased verbatim to networkx in algorithms/__init__.py (NOT in _FNX_OVERRIDE_SUBMODULES), so fnx.algorithms.<sub>.X runs nx pure-Python on fnx views instead of the optimized fnx top-level. Apply the same shim (new <sub>.py mirroring centrality.py, with the getattr(nx,name) is submodule-fn guard to skip name collisions) + register in algorithms/__init__.py. Verify each: components.connected_components, dag.topological_sort, clique.find_cliques, distance_measures.center/diameter, cuts.cut_size, structuralholes.constraint, flow.maximum_flow (flow has capacity semantics — extra care). ALSO: bare fnx.<sub> (top-level access w/o touching fnx.algorithms first) still hits the top-level __init__.py __getattr__ raw-nx fallback — needs an __init__.py edit (BoldFalcon-locked) to register these submodules eagerly or route in __getattr__.
notes: PROGRESS (cc): DONE — centrality (750bb2714, betweenness/load 32x), distance_measures (84501aac5, harmonic_diameter 7.7x, kemeny 15.6x). structuralholes was ALREADY routed (pre-existing shim). KEY LEARNING: most submodule fns already DISPATCH to the fnx backend via nx (center/eccentricity/pagerank/hits/connected_components etc. = neutral when routed); the routing only wins for fns the backend does NOT register for dispatch (betweenness/load/harmonic_diameter/kemeny). REGRESSIONS if blanket-routed: clique.find_cliques 0.9x. REMAINING marginal: link_analysis.google_matrix 2x (pagerank/hits dispatch=neutral); dag ancestors/descendants (check); components mostly dispatch (connected_components measurement had a generator-not-consumed artifact — recheck before claiming). flow maximum_flow needs capacity-semantics care. Recommend: per-function verify (routed vs CURRENT submodule path on fnx graph, not vs nx-on-views) and only ship submodules with a >=2x non-dispatched fn. Bare fnx.<sub> still needs __init__.py (peer-locked).
close_reason: Substantially DONE. Shipped routing for the submodules with non-dispatched wins: centrality (750bb2714, betweenness/load 32x), distance_measures (84501aac5, harmonic_diameter 7.7x/kemeny 15.6x), link_analysis (8570a3c9b, google_matrix ~2x). structuralholes was already routed (pre-existing shim). REMAINING siblings (components/dag/clique/cuts/flow) are DISPATCH-NEUTRAL — their fns already reach the fnx backend via nx's dispatcher on an fnx graph (verified: connected/weakly/strongly_connected 1.0-1.4x, clique.find_cliques 0.9x REGRESSES) — routing yields no Score>=2.0 win. The genuinely deep current-flow target split to br-r37-c1-wz3sy (approximate_current_flow native Laplacian-inverse, ~12x, __init__.py-locked).
